fact: June 5 is exactly 156 days into the year, which means there are only 209 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for fireflies to start their magical light shows in backyards and forests.
The sun rises super early around June 5 in places like Alaska - we're talking 4 AM or even earlier!
Cherry trees in many parts of the world are dropping their petals like pink and white snow right around this date.
June 5 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when the zodiac twins are supposedly making everyone extra chatty and curious.
Mosquitoes are hitting their annoying peak in many places by early June - they love the warm, humid weather that's settling in.
We're only about 2 weeks away from the summer solstice, so daylight is stretching longer and longer each day in the north.
In Australia and other southern places, June 5 means winter is settling in and leaves are turning brilliant orange and red.
Many flowers like roses and peonies are bursting into full bloom around this time, making gardens smell absolutely incredible.
The moon phase on June 5 changes every year, but when it's a new moon around this date, the night sky becomes perfect for stargazing.
Ocean water temperatures in many coastal areas are finally warming up enough by early June to make swimming feel amazing instead of freezing.
Baby animals born in spring are getting big and brave enough to venture out on their own around this time of year.
